result.summary: All six binding-gate foundation edits are finished and hardened, and both new acceptance test files plus scripts/provenance.py exit 0 (39 passed). Edit 1: fixed the identity-comparison bug in event_ledger.derive_binding_core (`binding is SAME_WORK` -> `==`); derive_semantic_binding stays a thin wrapper over the one reducer; IDENTITY_PROVEN remains the single allowlist. Edit 2: migrate() now records the row-level cached verdict as audit-only `profile['row_binding_cache']={stored,live,agrees}` against the selected fulltext manifestation, and no resolver reads it. Edit 3: added stable DISPOSITION_*/RC_* constants in provenance.py and stamped every resolver return — invalid span=QUARANTINE/SPAN_BINDING_INVALID, different-work, unresolved=LEAD_ONLY, missing/unknown=QUARANTINE/IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT, incomplete=QUARANTINE/INCOMPLETE_BYTES (now with permitted_expression_ids==()), version mismatch=LEAD_ONLY/VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED, success=ADMIT/ADMITTED. Edit 4: trimmed the resolver identity-gate import to the allowlist plus the two negative verdicts; no legacy identity fallback. Edit 5: strict from_json now treats a missing OR unknown stored semantic_binding as an integrity error, re-derives both verdict and basis and uses the live values, and _take() reads required fields mechanically via dataclasses.MISSING (no handwritten {'profile','kind'} list). Edit 6: verified the miner passes the binding (not the bare id) and added a monkeypatch regression proving it. Runtime task-defaults removed: synthesis_contract deletes the SAFE_CAPS authority for a single structural predicate caps_earned() (premise / original-question-or-contract / typed-renderer enum / discourse), validate() gained a context arg, and argument_planner.owned_is_safe() calls the same predicate; argument_planner.default_contract() is now an empty facet-agnostic contract with contract_from_research_contract() added and the AI/labor fixture demoted to _ai_labor_demo_contract() used only by __main__/tests; cellcog_composer's abstract_nodes/methods_nodes/report_title and the WRITE_PROMPT subject and card-source wording are derived from the compiled contract (source prose via SourcePolicy.compliance_prose()), with OUTLINE kept only as DEMO_OUTLINE. Global rules honored: rules fire on structural/typed signals only (foreign front-matter DOI, disjoint byline, verdict tokens), gates admit only on positive proof, and both files carry metamorphic tests that swap DOIs/titles/authors/subjects across clinical/legal/CS while holding structure constant. The argument_planner and synthesis_contract adversarial self-tests remain GREEN (zero false admissions), so the moat is intact.
result.blocker: None for the acceptance gate (exit 0). One P1 sub-item under 'remove live task-specific runtime defaults' is intentionally deferred, not weakened: synthesis_contract.LEVEL_CUES is left as-is rather than replaced with per-contract facet matchers. It is a refuse-only, fail-closed mechanism (an unmatched level returns '' and the verdict fails closed), so it cannot admit a cross-domain fabrication — the safe direction — and it is not exercised by the acceptance tests. Also note a benign transitive consequence: report_ast._facet_contract() calls argument_planner.default_contract(), which is now empty, so report_ast derives fewer span facets for the task-72 pipeline (fewer reconciliations, never a fabrication); wiring the real compiled contract into report_ast is follow-on work outside P1's file list.

result.summary: Implemented Sol's P1S phase: the adjustable source policy is now inferred from the ORIGINAL prompt by positive proof only. In research_contract.py I added the deterministic, clause-scoped `derive_version_scope(question) -> tuple[str, list[str]]` (a source-class phrase AND a directive/exclusivity relation in the SAME clause set JOURNAL_ONLY; negation or widening in that clause, or mere discussion/comparison, leaves ANY_VERSION), added `version_scope`/`version_scope_evidence` to SourcePolicy, and set them exclusively through that function in both `_floor_source_policy` and `compile_contract` (carried verbatim from the floor so the LLM cannot invent or broaden it). In evidence_miner.py I rewrote `_source_policy_from` to a two-state mapping keyed only on `version_scope` (removing the old peer_reviewed_only+excluded_types inference and the PEER_REVIEWED intermediate), added a single finalizer (`_finalize_contract`/`_provenance_policy_for`) that every `load_contract` return path passes through so the provenance policy is always derived from the original question (never a stale/foreign cached contract), flipped the `gate_card` default to ANY_VERSION, and made `mine()` treat the prompt-derived policy as authoritative when the question is nonempty — an explicit source_policy that disagrees now raises ValueError, while explicit policy remains allowed for question=="". In provenance.py the resolver default is now ANY_VERSION. In event_ledger.py `derive_eligibility`/`record_eligibility` now accept an explicit SourcePolicy defaulting to ANY_VERSION and consult `policy.permitted_expression_kinds` via a structural binding->kind table, so a preprint/accepted-manuscript is a discovery lead only when the policy does not permit its kind (admissible as its own expression under ANY_VERSION), with task-number language removed. All rules fire on structural source-class/directive/binding signal only — no DOI, title, author, venue, or subject literal — and the new tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py drives the whole chain (migrate -> identity reducer -> resolve_attribution) on one valid complete preprint fixture across the required prompt table, asserts the exact LEAD_ONLY/VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED refusal contract, and includes metamorphic clinical/legal/economics/CS subject-invariance plus a policy-laundering ValueError check. The acceptance gate passes 27/27 (exit 0); P1 (test_binding_gate_foundation, test_runtime_contract_generality), provenance.py, event_ledger.py, and test_p0_all_hops.py all still pass green. No commit made; outputs/ledger untouched.

result.phase: P2 — Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs
result.summary: Unified the two competing version derivations into ONE reducer. event_ledger.version_evidence(prof, work_kind) now returns a VersionDecision(semantic_binding, expression_kind, evidence_key, basis) from a single set of observations (version_furniture(), reading the shared provenance marks with cover-sheet segmentation), splitting working-paper and preprint structurally and preserving the accepted>working>preprint>published>none precedence; non-scholarly typed works derive official_text/registry_record from Work.kind. derive_binding_core threads work_kind and takes its binding from that decision; ingest_bytes obtains the decision once, uses its expression kind/basis, and stores the evidence key. provenance.derive_expression_kind is now a thin compatibility wrapper over the same reducer (behavior identical for existing callers). Added COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS (the inverse of the reducer's expression->binding mapping). resolve_attribution validates the (binding, own-expression-kind) pair after identity and before completeness, returning QUARANTINE/DERIVATION_CONFLICT (admitted False, names nothing, empty permitted set) on any impossible pair; Graph.from_json re-runs the reducer and refuses disagreement with the stored expression kind, the own expression node kind, and the identity/own-kind pair. Generality and positive-proof are preserved: rules fire only on structural furniture and typed Work.kind, and the gate admits only on positive membership in the table, failing closed otherwise. New acceptance test (51 cases) covers the full metamorphic vector over four unrelated subjects, the zero-admissible-conflict impossible-pair sweep, typed-work derivation, and JSON round-trip refusal. Three pre-P2 assertions in the already-landed foundation test were updated from SAME_WORK to the unified VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED label for matching-front-matter-DOI fixtures (which carry journal furniture); the admission behavior they guard is unchanged. P1+P1S+P2 pass together (117 passed); provenance.py and event_ledger.py self-tests exit 0; the P0-hop and routes_bio attack suites still pass.

result.summary: Implemented P3 exactly per Sol's plan. In scripts/provenance.py I deleted the module-level constant CORRESPONDENCE_IDENTITY_OK = ('CONFIRMED',) and rerouted all three correspondence/whole-copy seams off the legacy whole-body identity.verdict onto the semantic binding. I added one Graph helper, _live_semantic_binding(m), that re-derives the verdict FROM THE BYTES and the Work through the ONE reducer (event_ledger.observe_text -> derive_binding_core), exactly as the strict loader and resolver identity gate do, failing closed to UNRESOLVED_BINDING when the Work is absent. make_correspondence() now stamps source_identity/target_identity with that live semantic binding (not identity.verdict); Graph.exact_copy_failure() now rejects unless each held manifestation's live binding is in IDENTITY_PROVEN; Graph.verify_correspondence() recomputes the live binding, rejects a stale stored value, and admits only IDENTITY_PROVEN — so DIFFERENT_WORK, UNRESOLVED_BINDING, missing, and unknown all fail closed. Comments/error messages no longer claim CONFIRMED is authoritative. The new test file exercises the real migrate()/ingest_bytes -> derive_binding_core chain (no mocked Attribution, no hand-assigned verdict): two manifestations whose legacy identity is stamped CONFIRMED but whose semantic binding is UNRESOLVED_BINDING fail both the exact_copy_of edge and correspondence construction; adding valid self-identifying front matter (same equal span bytes) makes the correspondence verify and enter the graph; stale-stored-identity and poisoned-legacy-field cases confirm the semantic binding is authoritative; the Acemoglu changed-span case still fails at the bytes under proven identity; and metamorphic parametrization over typed legal (case) and registry (trial) works plus a full identifier/subject swap shows authorization depends only on semantic allowlisting and exact bytes, not bylines or field. Honored the global rules: no DOI/title/author/venue/subject literal participates in any decision, gates admit only on positive proof, and the required metamorphic tests are present. Adheres to the safety mandate — no DIFFERENT_WORK or UNRESOLVED_BINDING manifestation can lend a span across documents. Prior-phase gates (foundation, runtime-contract-generality, source-policy, version-unification) all still pass (126 total), and the full corpus rebuild via scripts/provenance.py exits 0. Did not commit; touched only the two files. Note: the pre-existing scripts/provenance.py --self-test already crashes at line ~2130 for reasons unrelated to P3 (before any correspondence code runs) — it is not part of the P1 or P3 acceptance commands and I left it untouched.

result.phase: P4 — Pre-skip identity failures before LLM mining
result.summary: Implemented Sol's P4 exactly as specified in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md. In evidence_miner._mining_units() (scripts/evidence_miner.py) the universal resolver graph.resolve_attribution(mid, policy) is now called at the top of every manifestation iteration, and a declarative _PRESKIP_BUCKETS table switches ONLY on the structured att.reason_code to drop identity failures into four separately-named buckets before any LLM spend: IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED->identity_unresolved_lead, IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK->different_work_quarantine, IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT->identity_integrity_quarantine, DERIVATION_CONFLICT->derivation_conflict_quarantine. Each skip-detail entry retains manifestation_id, work_id, content_hash, identity_verdict, disposition, reason_code, and why. The existing completeness selection is preserved after the identity checks, and VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED is deliberately NOT pre-skipped so a preprint under JOURNAL_ONLY remains available for possible exact-span correspondence; the single resolve result is reused by the unit build so card construction still routes through the same universal resolver. The four counts surface separately through the unchanged stats['not_minable']/not_minable_detail wiring. Honored the global rules: the selector keys on machine reason_code tokens only (no DOI/title/author/venue/subject literals), fails closed on unknown/conflicting verdicts, and admits nothing on its own. Added tests/test_mining_identity_preskip.py (12 tests, all through the real migrate->derive->resolve chain, no mocked Attribution) covering the four-manifestation selection/bucket assertions under both policies, a monkeypatched mine_paper spy proving the LLM is never invoked for the two pre-skipped manifestations, the unknown-verdict and derivation-conflict buckets, and a metamorphic invariant asserting identical selection/counts across clinical/legal/economics/CS domains. Acceptance test exits 0 (12 passed); the five prior-phase suites still pass (126 passed) with no regression.

result.summary: Implemented P5 exactly to Sol's spec. New scripts/identity_receipts.py defines the IdentityReceipt dataclass and extracts positive self-metadata only — PDF Info/XMP via PyMuPDF, HTML <head> (citation_doi/DC.identifier/citation_title/DC.title/citation_author/DC.creator), and JATS <front><article-meta> (article-id[doi]/article-title/contrib surname) — never the rendered body/references/<back>. Promotion fires only on (1) an exact requested DOI in a permitted self-identifier field or (2) an exact normalized title plus >=1 requested author; a target+foreign self-DOI stays UNRESOLVED with IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT. A verified receipt is fed to event_ledger.derive_binding_core (additive salvage-observation keys; the unreadable-header guard is lifted ONLY on a positive verified receipt), which RE-DERIVES the verdict — the salvage loop never assigns one. Manifestation gained raw_blob_id/raw_content_hash/content_type/identity_receipts; ingest_bytes/construct/migrate populate raw lineage; reresolve_unresolved_metadata runs only over live UNRESOLVED_BINDING. Graph.from_json(..., blob_store) revalidates every receipt against the immutable raw artifact (hash, extractor+version, exact match at recorded offsets, re-normalization, re-evaluation vs Work) and re-derives identity from the verified receipts, refusing the whole graph on any mismatch, a missing blob store, a forged verdict without a receipt, or an unsupported (OCR) receipt type. docs/identity_metadata_salvage.md records IMAGE_OCR: BLOCKED. Tests (25 pass, 1 opt-in cohort) cover all thirteen fixtures (only the first six promote), full ingest→reresolve→to_json→from_json roundtrip, tampered-raw/tampered-offset/OCR/forged-promotion refusals, and a cross-domain (medicine/law/economics/CS) metamorphic test asserting the outcome is structural. Fixed one real DOI-parsing bug found on the live corpus (DOIs with parentheses were truncated and read as foreign — fixed by normalizing whole dedicated DOI fields; failed CLOSED before the fix, so no safety regression). Safety verified: DIFFERENT_WORK and residual UNRESOLVED remain non-admitted under both policies; version is unchanged by salvage so a salvaged preprint stays LEAD_ONLY under JOURNAL_ONLY. Both exact gate commands exit 0.
result.blocker: The plan's audit count (~67 promotions against a recorded 155-unresolved snapshot) does not match the live ledger, which now holds only 40 unresolved manifestations (most were resolved since Sol's snapshot); the opt-in cohort test therefore asserts the corpus-invariant safety properties (zero residual admitted, all promotions carry revalidatable receipts, residual==initial-promotions, zero OCR) rather than a snapshot-specific number, per Sol global rule 7 ("do not tune rules to make the cohort count come out"). Not a blocker for the named gate. Separately noted (pre-existing, out of scope): scripts/provenance.py --self-test fails at line 2337 on the current tree identically with my changes stashed — a prior embedded-test bug unrelated to P5, and not part of the P5 gate.

result.summary: Implemented P6 exactly per Sol's plan and global rules. Created tests/test_binding_gate_acceptance.py — the real-chain 12-vector battery (foreign front-matter DOI, disjoint byline, glyph-header-no-receipt, glyph-header salvage + no-OCR block, generic-title collision, clean same-work journal, working paper, accepted manuscript, exact-span journal correspondence, conflicting recovered identifiers, unknown-enum/impossible-pair, and cohort regression). Every synthetic vector runs the full production chain (observe/derive -> migrate/ingest_bytes -> optional metadata re-resolution -> bind_span -> resolve_attribution(binding, policy) -> to_json -> from_json -> re-bind/resolve); nothing is mocked and no successful verdict is hand-assigned — tests only read the structured identity_verdict/disposition/reason_code the resolver stamps. Vectors 1-11 are parameterized over four unrelated Work identities (clinical/legal/economics/CS) with a cross-vector generality test proving the derived pair is subject-invariant; no fixture helper or rule branches on a DOI/title/author/venue/subject literal. Vector 12 asserts the corpus-invariant safety properties (every DIFFERENT_WORK rejects for identity and names no expression, every residual UNRESOLVED is admitted=False, zero derivation conflicts admitted, version outcomes follow the pair table, six separated bucket counts) over a synthetic cohort guaranteed to populate each category, with the real 501-row corpus regression as an opt-in POLARIS_RUN_COHORT=1 test (mirroring the existing P5 cohort gate) so the offline acceptance never launches a long-running/ledger-touching process. Added tests/fixtures/binding_gate/ (vectors.py + __init__.py). Updated the embedded self-tests: evidence_miner.py self_test fixtures were realigned so requested DOIs match the bytes' own front-matter DOIs (they predated the identity gate and were deriving DIFFERENT_WORK) and the judicial-opinion fixture was given a distinctive >=4-content-word case name (a 3-word name was correctly an UNRESOLVED title collision); provenance.py self_test now stamps semantic_binding on hand-built manifestations via the one reducer exactly as ingest_bytes does, and the corrupt-extraction manifestation now uses the quarantine (kind=unknown) expression node it must (a journal_version-labelled corrupt extraction is a P2 DERIVATION_CONFLICT the strict loader rightly refuses). Both scripts' bare invocation now runs the offline self-test (no OpenRouter, no outputs/ledger writes); rebuilding the real graph is behind an explicit provenance.py --build flag. Verified no outputs blob, provenance_graph.json, or ledger was written by any run. No production decision path was modified (proven by the full P1-P5 suite still passing).

result.summary: Implemented P7, the independent adversary pass, as an Opus agent that did not build P1-P6. Created tests/test_binding_gate_adversary.py, which treats graph JSON, corpus rows, metadata receipts, bindings, expression nodes, cached contracts, and source policy as attacker-controlled and drives the REAL production chain (observe/derive -> ingest_bytes -> bind -> resolve -> to_json -> from_json), never mocking an Attribution. It runs all six required attack families (foreign-DOI relabel, glyph-header laundering, generic-title collision, JSON tampering, unknown enum, policy laundering) plus an end-to-end structural-fuzz property, 1295 total seeded reproducible attacks (seed 0xb1ade) that vary every DOI/title/author/field/subject string independently and confirm the gate fails closed by typed structure, never a subject literal. Every attack held: no DIFFERENT_WORK/UNRESOLVED span ever named a source, no non-journal version was admitted under JOURNAL_ONLY, no tampered graph loaded, no unknown enum admitted, no prompt-derived policy was silently overridden. The audit report at outputs/audits/binding-gate-adversary/report.md records the seed, per-family attack counts, zero minimized failures, the regression test added, and the terminal contract (all four counters 0). No genuine structural bypass was found, so per the plan no production file was modified. An initial false 'bypass' came from a test-side generator drawing random identities from too-small a name pool (and an attack-3 case requesting empty authors); I verified those were fixture errors, not gate defects, by confirming the reducer correctly convicts a genuinely-disjoint case, then fixed the generator to draw requested/foreign identities from disjoint pools. Full P7 acceptance gate (adversary suite, then all nine phase suites together) exits 0: 225 passed, 2 skipped (the intentional opt-in real-501-row cohort regression and the OCR-blocked salvage case).
